commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Human review should decide the fallback-removal tradeoff and require real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for a repair worker from this review.; Cleared: The diff narrows core session delivery routing and updates tests; it does not change dependencies, CI, secrets handling, or package execution surfaces. (sha=5d94ad20d23e609fecb99076af076fa1bd840df1)
commands.review_summary:
The branch removes the run-scoped active-run durable-session fallback from sessions_send and updates regression tests to require an error with no fallback agent call when active queue delivery is rejected.

PR surface: Source -29, Tests -41. Total -70 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the focused root path: current main and v2026.6.1 show rejected active-run queue sends can fall back to a durable session, and the PR regression targets that branch. The reporter's full macOS fleet corruption loop was not live-reproduced in this review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review should decide the fallback-removal tradeoff and require real behavior proof; there is no narrow code defect for a repair worker from this review.

target.title: fix #91420: [Bug]: Delivery retry loop corrupts active sessions (R-004) — retry selector bypasses delivery.mode=none
target.body:
## Summary

- Stops `sessions_send` from retrying a rejected run-scoped active delivery by starting a new run on the durable session key.
- Keeps successful active-run queue delivery intact, including the best-effort path when transcript commit waiting is unsupported.
- Surfaces the active-run queue rejection on the original run-scoped target instead of using an unrelated persistent session as a relay.
- Intentionally does not change documented agent-to-agent reply-back behavior, channel delivery, or session visibility policy.
- Review focus: the `startAgentRun` rejection path and the updated active-run regression coverage.

## Linked context

Closes #91420

Related: ClawSweeper issue review identified the unsafe `sessions_send` active-run fallback/A2A path as the focused repair target.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No direct maintainer request; this follows the open bug report and bot review guidance on the issue.

## Maintainer review notes

- Fix classification: root-cause fix for the active-run delivery state invariant.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High; the diff is limited to the rejected active-run send path plus focused regression coverage, and the patched gateway starts cleanly in a real local runtime.
- Root cause: `sessions_send` treated an active-run queue rejection as a reason to derive the durable session key and start a separate `agent` run, so a failed run-scoped delivery could cross the session lifecycle boundary and target an unrelated persistent session.
- Why this is root-cause fix: the active run queue is the source-of-truth delivery boundary for a run-scoped session key; when that queue rejects the message, the correct invariant is to surface the queue failure on the original target instead of resolving a different session and masking the state error.
- Why it matters / User impact: rejected retry delivery no longer risks duplicate relay messages, polluted durable sessions, or later provider/tool payload confusion in multi-agent message delivery flows.
- What did NOT change: out of scope are normal direct `sessions_send`, successful active-run queue delivery, documented A2A reply-back behavior, channel delivery policy, session visibility, provider routing, schema, config, defaults, and migrations.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: the change stays inside the `sessions_send` runtime state boundary and does not alter public schema, provider routing, config defaults, or migration behavior; the run queue remains canonical for run-scoped delivery.
- Related open PR / competition scan: daily-fix candidate checks found no linked open PR for #91420 before this branch was initialized, so this is not a same-contract competing PR.
- Target test file: `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts`; adjacent guardrails also run `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts` and `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-helpers.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: rejected active-run queue delivery returns an error for the original run-scoped session key, does not start a durable-session `agent` call, and does not report `fallback_failed`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression asserts the exact state transition that previously leaked across session boundaries while preserving the existing successful queue-delivery and A2A reply-back coverage.
- Patch quality notes: the word `fallback` appears in test names and assertions only to describe the removed unsafe path; this is not a mitigation, catch-all default, compatibility shim, or symptom-masking workaround.
- Risk labels considered: availability and message-delivery.
- Risk explanation: the highest-risk area is message-delivery availability for callers that accidentally depended on the unsafe durable-session reroute after active-run queue rejection.
- Why acceptable: the new failure mode is explicit and scoped to rejected run-scoped queue delivery; successful active queue sends, normal session sends, and A2A reply-back behavior remain covered by the focused validation set.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: rejected active-run `sessions_send` delivery no longer falls back through the durable session key, avoiding duplicate relay delivery into unrelated persistent sessions.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw dev gateway started from this patch with an isolated runtime home and a redacted gateway token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Started `gateway:dev` from the patched source with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`.
  - Probed the running gateway `/readyz` and `/healthz` endpoints.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  Copied live output from the running gateway:

  ```text
  === GATEWAY READY ===
  [gateway] http server listening
  [gateway] ready
  Control UI: http://127.0.0.1:19001
  Token: <redacted>
  ```

  ```text
  /readyz status=200 body={"ready":true,"failing":[]}
  /healthz status=200 body={"ok":true,"status":"live"}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the patched gateway reached ready state and answered live health checks from the isolated runtime; no secrets were exposed in the captured output.
- What was not tested: the reporter's full macOS 16-agent fleet, external channel accounts, and the intermittent production corruption loop.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the local run proves the patched gateway boots and serves health in a real runtime; the issue-specific active-run rejection branch is covered in the validation section below.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the production change, the focused reproduction returned `accepted` instead of `error`, showing the durable-session fallback was still active.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-helpers.test.ts
git diff --check
local gateway health probe: /readyz and /healthz
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Updated the run-scoped active delivery rejection test to require an error instead of durable-session reroute.
- Added assertions that the rejected active-run path does not start a fallback `agent` call and does not report `fallback_failed`.
- Preserved coverage that successful active-run queue delivery still avoids fallback agent runs.

What failed before this fix:

```text
FAIL sessions_send reports active-run queue rejection without durable-session fallback
AssertionError: expected 'accepted' to be 'error'
```

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — rejected run-scoped active delivery now reports an error instead of silently retrying through a durable session.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes — tool execution routing is narrowed so a failed active-run queue delivery cannot use a persistent session as an implicit relay.

What is the highest-risk area? Existing workflows that accidentally depended on the unsafe durable-session fallback will now see an explicit send error.

How is that risk mitigated? The change only affects rejected run-scoped active queue delivery; normal direct `sessions_send`, successful active-run queue delivery, transcript-commit best-effort delivery, and documented A2A reply-back behavior remain covered by focused tests.

## Current review state

What is the next action? Ready for maintainer review after CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? CI has not run until this PR is submitted. External reporter-environment proof is not available from this local setup.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Addresses ClawSweeper's issue guidance to repair the `sessions_send` active-run fallback/A2A path without removing documented reply-back behavior.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
